science.title: Scientists discovered the brain doesn't make decisions the way we thought
science.summary: A new study suggests the brain begins making decisions much earlier than scientists previously thought. Researchers found that even primary sensory regions are influenced by higher brain areas through rapid feedback loops, rather than simply passing information forward. This more dynamic view of brain function could he

science.title: A vitamin A discovery is changing what scientists know about vision
science.summary: A surprising discovery is reshaping scientists' understanding of how humans develop sharp central vision before birth. Instead of blue cone cells migrating away from the retina's center, the study found they transform into red and green cones under the influence of vitamin A-related signals and thyroid hormones. The fi

science.title: A hidden immune backup system could supercharge mRNA cancer vaccines
science.summary: Researchers found that mRNA cancer vaccines can recruit an unexpected immune cell to launch powerful tumor-fighting responses, overturning a long-held assumption about how the vaccines work. The discovery could lead to more effective cancer vaccines and help scientists tailor treatments for better patient outcomes.

science.title: Harvard scientists turn a silicon chip into a DNA writing machine
science.summary: Scientists have created a silicon chip that can write dozens of DNA sequences simultaneously using electricity and water-based enzymes, offering a cleaner alternative to conventional DNA manufacturing. The breakthrough could eventually support portable DNA-writing devices and even massive DNA data storage, although new

science.title: Microscope Breakthrough Could Reveal Proteins Once Thought Too Small To See
science.summary: A laser-based innovation could allow electron microscopes to reveal biological structures that have long remained beyond their reach. Most human proteins remain too small for today’s most powerful biological microscopes to resolve clearly. A laser system developed by UC Berkeley physicists could help bring many of them

tech.title: The Download: worms fight pollution, and geoengineering faces reality
tech.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. tech.title: Beatbot AquaSense X Review: A Pool Robot That Cleans Itself
tech.summary: The AquaSense X brings self-cleaning technology to pool robots for the first time, but is it worth nearly twice the price of Beatbot’s flagship cleaner?

tech.title: The Download: a nuclear landmark, and China eyes Nvidia chips

tech.title: Hermes agent maker Nous Research in talks for new funding at $1.5B valuation
tech.summary: The company is raising at least $75 million, led by Robot, with significant participation from USV and other prominent investors.

tech.title: Uber’s robotaxi lobbying effort puts it on a collision course with Waymo
tech.summary: Washington, D.C. has become a battleground for Uber and Waymo's competing views.

medicine.title: Ibuprofen safely dampens inflammatory markers in 28-patient drug-resistant tuberculosis trial
medicine.summary: The Experimental Tuberculosis Unit (UTE) at the Germans Trias i Pujol Research Institute (IGTP) has published in Nature Communications the results of a phase IIa pilot clinical trial assessing ibuprofen as a host-directed therapy in patients with pre-extensively drug-resistant tuberculosis (pre-XDR-TB) and extensively

medicine.title: Common blood pressure drug could make cancer therapy far more powerful
medicine.summary: Researchers found that the common blood pressure drug telmisartan can significantly improve the performance of the cancer drug olaparib, potentially expanding its benefits beyond patients with BRCA-related tumors. The combination is already being tested in human clinical trials after showing strong immune-boosting and

medicine.title: Study finds obesity 'fuels' leukemia, but a combo using popular weight-loss drugs may stop it
medicine.summary: Obesity can act as fuel for leukemia, according to a study led by Indiana University School of Medicine scientists. To help patients facing aggressive blood cancers overcome this metabolic risk, researchers identified a potential treatment strategy that combines popular weight-loss medications with anti-inflammatory dr

medicine.title: Experimental drug reverses severe fatty liver disease by repairing the gut
medicine.summary: An experimental drug called DT-109 reversed severe fatty liver disease in animal studies by repairing the gut and preventing harmful toxins from damaging the liver. The discovery could open the door to a new class of treatments for MASH and potentially other diseases tied to gut health.

medicine.title: A natural compound steps into the estrogen arena
medicine.summary: Uterine fibroids and endometriosis are two of the most common gynecologic diseases, affecting 15% to 80% of women of childbearing age. Existing treatments—hormonal drugs and selective estrogen receptor modulators—have side effects and cannot reliably prevent recurrence. This is why less harmful non-hormonal therapies a

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win 2026 Breakthrough, New Horizons prizes
mathematics.summary: Faculty member Shu-Heng Shao, in addition to four MIT alumni, are honored by the Breakthrough Prize Foundation.

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: Seven Perfect Shuffles Randomize a Deck of Cards. But How Many Sloppy Ones?
mathematics.summary: A decades-old proof showed that seven shuffles are enough to mix up a deck of cards. But it requires you to cut the deck with the precision of a professional magician. A new proof gets around that obstacle. The post Seven Perfect Shuffles Randomize a Deck of Cards. But How Many Sloppy Ones? first appeared on Quanta Mag

mathematics.title: How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math
mathematics.summary: With automated proof-checkers, a problem can be broken up into small chunks, solved bit-by-bit, then reassembled with confidence that every piece is correct. For some, this heralds a new area in mathematical research. The post How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up
mathematics.summary: By replacing the most fundamental concept in topology, Peter Scholze and Dustin Clausen are taking the first step in a far bigger program to understand why numbers behave the way they do. The post Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up first appeared on Quanta Magazine

space.title: Getting Vikram-1 to orbit: Inside Skyroot Aerospace's coming bid to make spaceflight history
space.summary: Backed by a $1.1 billion valuation, Skyroot Aerospace is betting that its Vikram-1 rocket can help transform India's commercial launch industry. And Vikram-1 is set to fly soon.

space.title: The FCC just gave Reflect Orbital permission to launch its 1st space mirror to orbit. Tens of thousands more could follow
space.summary: Reflect Orbital just got approval to launch its first light-directing space mirror, which it plans to do later this year. Tens of thousands more such craft could follow over the next 10 years.

space.title: Space medicine breakthrough? Kidney and liver tissue bioprinted off Earth for 1st time ever
space.summary: California-based company Auxilium Biotechnologies just produced kidney and liver tissue in space for the first time, using a method called bioprinting,

space.title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launches for 35th time, hauls Starlink satellites to orbit (video)
space.summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket flew for the 35th time tonight (July 10), carrying 29 Starlink satellites to low Earth orbit.

space.title: Waxing Gibbous Moon
space.summary: The waxing gibbous moon is nestled in the darkness of space in this June 26, 2026, image from the International Space Station. The space station was 264 miles above the Indian Ocean southeast of Madagascar at the time. The waxing gibbous phase comes before the full moon phase. During this time, the Moon appears brighte

astronomy.title: Astronomers Use a Neutron Star Merger to Measure Cosmic Expansion
astronomy.summary: Swinburne University of Technology and CSIRO have combined telescope and gravitational wave data in an attempt to unlock the true value of the Universe's expansion. Existing measurements of the Hubble Constant have split cosmologists for more than a decade.

astronomy.title: Steamy Nights at the Galactic Equator
astronomy.summary: Here's an observing guide to both familiar and lesser-known deep-sky objects that inhabit our galaxy's equatorial zone. The post Steamy Nights at the Galactic Equator appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Did a Passing Star Shower Us with Comets?
astronomy.summary: We might be living through a comet shower created by a star that millions of years ago jostled the Oort Cloud surrounding the solar system. The post Did a Passing Star Shower Us with Comets? appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Hayabusa 2 Completes Flyby Past Asteroid Torifune
astronomy.summary: Japan’s Hayabusa 2 mission has revealed another "snowman" asteroid — a pair of asteroids attached with a narrow neck. The contact binary could help shed light on planet formation. The post Hayabusa 2 Completes Flyby Past Asteroid Torifune appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Chemistry Reveals the Origins of an Interstellar Comet
astronomy.summary: Somewhere in the Milky Way Galaxy is an old star that has lost one of its comets. By some quirk of orbital mechanics, that frozen nucleus of ice and dust got kicked out of its home system and into a long and winding trajectory across space. It entered our Solar System sometime in the distant past and traveled somewhat

architecture.title: Rivers Before Roads: How Southeast Asia's Waterways Produced an Alternative Urbanism
architecture.summary: For most of the twentieth century, architecture has learned to read cities through roads. Street hierarchies define urban plans, intersections organize movement, and buildings are understood by the façades they present to sidewalks. Roads appear so fundamental to urban life that they are often mistaken for a universal

architecture.title: Teatro Mauri Restoration Preserves a 1951 Modernist Landmark in Valparaíso, Chile
architecture.summary: In June 2026, the refurbished Teatro Mauri reopened its doors on Cerro Bellavista in Valparaíso, formerly Chile's main port. architecture.title: Sants Social Center / LAOS (Xavier Botet + Albert Saboya)
architecture.summary: The rehabilitation project is based on the premise of placing people, the neighbourhood, activities, and social life at the centre of architecture. architecture.title: Primrose House / Robust Architecture Workshop
architecture.summary: Located within a dense suburban neighborhood in Kandy, Primrose House transforms a deteriorating two-bedroom dwelling into a light-filled home. Rather than demolishing the existing structure, the project embraces adaptive reuse as both an ecological strategy and a social proposition, demonstrating how under-performing

architecture.title: KPMB reveals designs for David Geffen School of Drama at Yale University
architecture.summary: Canadian architecture studio KPMB has released renderings of the new Dramatic Arts Building at Yale University, establishing the first consolidated space for the David Geffen School of Drama since its inception in 1924. The 207,000-square-foot (19,230-square-metre) design will host staged performances of the Yale Reper

construction.title: Letter to the Editor: There's Nothing New Under the Sun for Bridges
construction.summary: I get a bit amused by articles on “new” construction methods for bridges, such as the following one in Ohio.

construction.title: Micron pours first concrete at $100B New York chip fab
construction.summary: The chipmaker announced the milestone more than one quarter ahead of the original schedule on the megaproject that includes Gilbane, Bechtel and Jacobs.

construction.title: How BIM is becoming construction’s most effective margin protection strategy
construction.summary: Along with design support, BIM is also becoming one of construction’s smartest margin plays.

construction.title: Acciona to acquire Georgia infrastructure firm
construction.summary: The deal, which is expected to close near the end of the year, will help the Spanish infrastructure stalwart grow its presence in the U.S.
